skill.name: dev-tools-skill
skill.description: Guides AI agents through Chrome DevTools MCP workflows for debugging web app UI, console, network, and performance issues.
skill.summary: Debug web app issues with structured Chrome DevTools MCP workflows.
skill.author: Eccentric-jamaican

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives caused by Markdown inline code names for Chrome DevTools MCP commands. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections are also false positives from ordinary prose. The main residual risk is legitimate browser inspection through evaluate_script and network payload review, which can expose sensitive page data if used carelessly.

medium_findings.title: Browser Script Evaluation Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use evaluate_script for DOM inspection and layout measurement. This is a legitimate DevTools workflow, but it can execute JavaScript in the active page context if the agent goes beyond the read-only guidance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly direct use of evaluate_script. The skill also states read-only DOM queries and cautions against mutation, so this is a controlled capability rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Network Debugging May Expose Sensitive Data
low_findings.description: The skill asks agents to inspect request URLs, headers, payloads, response data, timing, and authentication-related failures. This is normal debugging context, but captured headers or payloads may contain sensitive user or session data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly instruct network request and payload review. The risk depends on the target application data, and there is no evidence of exfiltration or unauthorized collection.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings are Markdown inline code references to MCP tool names, not Ruby or shell execution. No evidence found of shell command execution in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown documentation entries for DevTools MCP commands. They do not contain executable scripts, shell wrappers, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Crypto And Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings arise from ordinary words and debugging recommendations, including Chrome DevTools wording and performance advice. No evidence found of cryptographic operations or host reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines are prose, not code. There are no weak hash functions, encryption calls, or system discovery commands in the cited content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Potential Page-Context JavaScript Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references evaluate_script for browser diagnostics. Use should remain read-only unless the user explicitly approves page mutations or form actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The capability is explicitly documented and can be risky if misused. The surrounding guidance limits the intended use to inspection, lowering concern.

content.user_title: Debug Web Apps with DevTools MCP
content.value_statement: Frontend bugs often require browser evidence from the live page, console, network, and performance traces. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured DevTools MCP workflow for focused diagnosis.
actual_capabilities: Guides page selection, accessibility snapshots, screenshots, and DOM inspection through Chrome DevTools MCP.
Provides workflows for UI interaction bugs, console errors, network failures, and performance lag.
Directs agents to gather evidence before proposing code changes.
Covers request status, headers, payload shape, timing, and CORS failure diagnosis.
Includes performance trace steps for LCP, FCP, CLS, long tasks, and large assets.
Encourages minimal, reversible fixes with validation steps.
limitations: Requires an available Chrome DevTools MCP server and access to the target page.
Does not implement fixes automatically unless the host agent can edit the project files.
Cannot diagnose server-side causes without related logs, API details, or reproduction context.
Browser inspection may expose sensitive request or page data, so users should review shared context.

use_cases.title: Fix Broken UI Interactions
use_cases.description: Use snapshots, screenshots, DOM inspection, and controlled interactions to locate layout, click, hover, and missing text issues.

use_cases.title: Investigate Failed API Calls
use_cases.description: Review network status, request headers, payloads, response bodies, timing, and console errors to isolate client-side failures.

use_cases.title: Triage Slow Web Pages
use_cases.description: Run browser performance traces and connect Core Web Vitals issues to large assets, long tasks, and layout shifts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for an unclear page problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the DevTools skill to inspect my web app. Start with the current page, collect console and network evidence, then tell me the most likely issue.

prompt_templates.title: UI Bug Diagnosis
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for visual or interaction bugs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the DevTools skill to debug this UI issue: [describe issue]. Reproduce it, inspect the affected element, and propose the smallest safe fix.

prompt_templates.title: Network Failure Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for API or data loading failures.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the DevTools skill to investigate why this request fails: [endpoint or feature]. Check status, headers, payload shape, timing, and console errors.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Trace Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for slow load or runtime lag.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the DevTools skill to run a performance trace for [page or flow]. Identify the top bottleneck and recommend prioritized fixes with validation steps.

output_examples.input: A user reports that a checkout button is visible but does not respond.
output: Observed the button in the accessibility snapshot and reproduced the failed click.
Found an overlapping element with higher stacking order blocking pointer events.
Recommended a minimal CSS fix and a validation step for desktop and mobile widths.

output_examples.input: A dashboard loads without data after deployment.
output: Checked network requests and found the dashboard API returning an authorization error.
Compared the request headers with the expected authentication flow.
Recommended fixing the token header path and adding a clear empty-state error message.

output_examples.input: A product page feels slow on first load.
output: Ran a performance trace with reload and reviewed the top Core Web Vitals insight.
Found large image assets and delayed rendering work as the main contributors.
Recommended responsive image sizing, deferred scripts, and a follow-up trace.

best_practices: Confirm user intent before inspecting private pages, submitting forms, or changing page state.
Collect browser evidence before proposing fixes, then connect each recommendation to observed data.
Keep evaluate_script usage read-only unless the user explicitly approves a mutation.
anti_patterns: Do not submit forms, accept dialogs, or trigger destructive actions without explicit confirmation.
Do not guess from source code alone when live browser evidence is available.
Do not paste sensitive headers, tokens, or payload data into summaries unless necessary.

faq.question: What does this skill help debug?
faq.answer: It helps debug UI bugs, console errors, failed network requests, and browser performance issues in web apps.

faq.question: Does it require Chrome DevTools MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflows assume the agent can use Chrome DevTools MCP commands against an accessible browser page.

faq.question: Can it fix code automatically?
faq.answer: It can recommend minimal fixes. Actual code changes depend on the host agent and project access.

faq.question: Is it safe for production pages?
faq.answer: It can inspect production pages, but users should avoid exposing sensitive headers, tokens, or private payloads.

faq.question: How does it handle performance issues?
faq.answer: It guides trace capture, insight review, network correlation, and prioritized recommendations for load and runtime bottlenecks.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is legitimate, but browser script evaluation and network inspection can expose sensitive data if used carelessly.
